source_quote: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. Probe R49 for input voltage with DMM; if absent, check F1 for continuity in diode-mode/resistance. Replace F1 if open (SMD fuse).

evidence.reasoning: Quote literally names F1 as the input fuse; F1 is in the FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP and exists in the graph.
Quote describes F1 as open (blown SMD fuse), mapping to mode=open.

evidence.source_quote_substring: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity
evidence.reasoning: F1 is source_refdes of VIN per the graph; if F1 opens, VIN dies and the whole board has no rail activity — consistent with the symptom.

source_quote: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. Measure J22 pin 15 for 3.3 V; if absent, probe U14 output in diode-mode for short.

evidence.reasoning: U14 mentioned literally; FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP confirms U14 (always-on 3.3V regulator) maps to graph U14.
Failure of regulator with no output corresponds to dead mode.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U14 is source_refdes of LPC_VCC per the graph; loss of U14 cascades to LPC_VCC.

evidence.source_quote_substring: LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U18 is supplied by LPC_VCC sourced from U14; D11 is on the LPC_VCC rail. Both die when U14 dies.

source_quote: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC.

evidence.reasoning: U14 mentioned literally; FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP confirms U14 maps to graph U14.
Quote explicitly says U14's output is shorted, mapping to mode=shorted.
U14 sources LPC_VCC per the graph; shorted output collapses LPC_VCC.
D11 indicates LPC_VCC; with rail collapsed D11 is dark, and U18 (also on LPC_VCC) is unresponsive.

source_quote: SD card boot fails; system won't read SD card even though LPC and 3.3 V main rail are confirmed live. Likely cause: 1.8 V rail dead — level shifters U28 and U8 require both 3.3 V and 1.8 V to operate, blocking the SD interface. Probe C130 for 1.8 V; if absent, identify 1.8 V buck regulator in schematic (Power section), check for short on output in diode-mode before reflowing or replacing.

evidence.reasoning: C130 mentioned literally as the 1.8V test capacitor; matches graph C130 on VCCIO/1.8V net.
Quote describes shorted output on 1.8V rail; a leaky MLCC produces a partial short.

evidence.source_quote_substring: check for short on output in diode-mode
evidence.reasoning: Diode-mode short detection on a rail cap implies sub-kΩ resistance; chose 200Ω as typical leaky-short value.
Quote literally says the 1.8V rail is dead. Maps to VCCIO in the graph (C130 is on VCCIO decoupling).
U28 is a level shifter on the SD interface needing 1.8V; with rail dead, U28 is non-functional. U8 not in graph.

source_quote: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Probe C37 for 1.2 V first; if absent, diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short.

evidence.reasoning: C37 mentioned literally as the 1.2V test cap; matches graph C37 on +1V2 rail.

evidence.source_quote_substring: diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short
evidence.reasoning: Quote suggests cap shorting the rail; leaky_short captures partial-short MLCC failure.
Typical leaky MLCC measures a few hundred ohms; 200Ω chosen.
Quote explicitly states 1.2V rail dead; maps to graph rail +1V2.

evidence.source_quote_substring: TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10)
evidence.reasoning: U9 and U10 both need 1.2V; with rail collapsed both are dead.

source_quote: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge, or Wi-Fi antenna routing interference into display path. Under microscope inspect J24 ZIF pads for lifted contacts; re-seat or re-flow. reflow U10 if suspected cold joint.

evidence.source_quote_substring: reflow U10 if suspected cold joint
evidence.reasoning: U10 named literally; cold joint applies to U10. FUNCTIONAL MAP confirms U10 maps to graph U10.
Cold joint on a signal pin is electrically open — mode=open captures the broken DSI signal path; rails remain intact.

source_quote: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge. Under microscope inspect J24 ZIF pads for lifted contacts; re-seat or re-flow.

evidence.reasoning: J24 named literally; FUNCTIONAL MAP confirms J24 maps to graph J24.
Lifted ZIF pad creates an open signal connection on J24.

source_quote: LPC UART responds and D11 is lit, but main CPU module does not start after sending `1P` power-on command; D11 goes out and nothing further happens. Likely cause: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged or a downstream rail is shorted, causing immediate shutdown.

evidence.reasoning: Quote refers to LPC (alias of U18 per FUNCTIONAL MAP); maps to graph U18.

evidence.source_quote_substring: LPC GPIO lines to main power rail switchers damaged
evidence.reasoning: GPIO damage rendering the controller unable to sequence rails maps to dead U18.
